expectations.text: Identifies that database/sql I/O inside the domain Order type is a smell — the reason points outward at a concrete
expectations.evidence: 'I/O and an outside concrete living in the reason'; 'dependency arrow points the wrong way'

expectations.text: Recommends a repository-style interface (port) owned by the domain/reason
expectations.evidence: Repository interface declared in internal/order, 'the reason owns this interface'

expectations.text: Moves the SQL into an adapter/connection (e.g. internal/postgres), leaving the domain free of database/sql
expectations.evidence: internal/order/postgres/repository.go holds the INSERT; drop database/sql from order.go

expectations.text: Keeps the order-total business rule in the domain, not pushed into the adapter
expectations.evidence: Place() retains the total computation and empty-order check, no I/O

expectations.text: Notes the construction/wiring belongs in cmd (how it's run)
expectations.evidence: cmd/server/main.go opens DB, builds postgres repo, wires service
